stops.name: Raffles Hotel
stops.shortDescription: A stunning colonial-style luxury hotel established in 1887. Its Long Bar is world-famous as the exact birthplace of the Singapore Sling cocktail.

stops.name: CHIJMES
stops.shortDescription: Originally a 19th-century Catholic convent and girls' school, this beautifully restored Gothic complex is now a premier dining and entertainment destination.

stops.name: St Andrew's Cathedral
stops.shortDescription: Singapore's largest cathedral, standing gleaming white in the Civic District. Its walls were uniquely plastered using 'Madras chunam', a mixture including shell lime and egg whites.

stops.name: National Gallery Singapore
stops.shortDescription: Housed within two brilliantly joined national monuments: the former Supreme Court and City Hall. It holds the world's largest public collection of Southeast Asian modern art.

stops.name: The Arts House (Old Parliament)
stops.shortDescription: The oldest surviving government building in Singapore, originally built in 1827 as a private mansion. It served as the Parliament House until 1999 and is now a thriving arts venue.

stops.name: Asian Civilisations Museum
stops.shortDescription: Located beautifully by the Singapore River, this museum deeply explores the rich, pan-Asian artistic heritage of the diverse cultures that make up Singapore's society.

stops.name: The Fullerton Hotel
stops.shortDescription: A magnificent neoclassical landmark that originally housed Singapore's General Post Office. Its imposing Doric columns and sweeping staircases overlook the river mouth.

stops.name: Merlion Park
stops.shortDescription: Home to Singapore's national icon, a mythical creature with a lion's head and a fish's body. The massive statue continuously spouts water into the scenic Marina Bay.

stops.name: Esplanade - Theatres on the Bay
stops.shortDescription: A premier performing arts center instantly recognizable by its spiky aluminum sunshades. Locals affectionately refer to the twin domes as 'the Durians' due to their resemblance to the spiky fruit.

stops.name: The Helix Bridge
stops.shortDescription: A visually striking, twisting pedestrian bridge inspired by the geometric arrangement of human DNA. It offers spectacular, unobstructed panoramic views across Marina Bay.

stops.name: ArtScience Museum
stops.shortDescription: An architectural marvel designed to look like a giant lotus flower. It hosts highly immersive, interactive digital exhibitions blending art, science, culture, and technology.

stops.name: Marina Bay Sands
stops.shortDescription: An awe-inspiring integrated resort defined by three massive hotel towers capped by a boat-shaped SkyPark. It has become the defining centerpiece of the modern Singapore skyline.

stops.name: Gardens by the Bay (Flower Dome)
stops.shortDescription: A jaw-dropping horticultural attraction. The massive glass Flower Dome replicates a cool-dry Mediterranean climate and holds the Guinness record for the world's largest glass greenhouse.

stops.name: Supertree Grove
stops.shortDescription: A surreal forest of towering, man-made tree structures covered in living plants. At night, they come alive with the dazzling Garden Rhapsody light and sound show.
